Bridge Command documentation confirms arpa_on=1 enables ARPA radar feature; setup_task.sh line 25 sets default to 0 for agent to modify
Bridge Command documentation confirms full_radar=1 opens radar maximized; setup_task.sh line 26 sets default to 0

No documentation found confirming max_radar_range=72 is a valid Bridge Command setting. Standard maritime radar ranges are 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 6, 12, 24nm. Bridge Command documentation shows defaults of 12nm. 72nm is unusually high and unverified.
Task description line 2; coordinate bounds validated against known Solent region (Lee-on-Solent: 50.8017N, -1.2017W; Southampton: 50.909698N, -1.404351W)

pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is a real, open-source 3D maritime simulator (GitHub: bridgecommand/bc)

pi_items.metadata_value: Supports ARPA radar, scenario configuration, multiple vessel modeling
pi_items.verified_value: Confirmed - Bridge Command documentation shows environment.ini, ownship.ini, othership.ini files and ARPA/radar features

privileged_info_summary: The task is based on synthetic AIS traffic data generated by setup_task.sh containing 8 unique vessels (EVER FORTUNE, RED OSPREY, WHITCHALLENGER, RED JET 7, SOLENT PILOT, MISCHIEF, GRAND PIONEER, COASTGUARD ECHO) with realistic positions and trajectories within the Solent maritime region (50.70-50.90N, -1.45--1.00W). The CSV contains 18 position records across 10 columns (MMSI, VesselName, VesselType, Latitude, Longitude, SOG_kts, COG, Heading, Timestamp, NavStatus). All metadata claims about vessel counts, vessel identities, and paths are accurate. Bridge Command is a verified real open-source maritime simulator supporting the required ARPA and radar configuration options (arpa_on=1, full_radar=1), though the max_radar_range=72 setting could not be verified in Bridge Command documentation. The ownship reference (MV Dorado at 50.78N, -1.15W) is within Solent bounds.
